A.error_quality: structured - E001 'missing contract block' prints a full worked requires()/ensures()/effects() example; E130 slot errors state how many bindings of a type are in scope and the valid index range; E500 prints SMT counterexamples. Caveat: the anonymous param-binder terminal renders as __ANON_0 in 'Expected one of' lists (the biggest source of confusion).
A.notes: Invariant enforcement = AI hand-written/structured (NOT machine-verified in the final runnable code). Recovered Vera's positional, type-indexed (De Bruijn) model entirely from compiler errors with NO guide. The >=0 guarantee comes from structure: deposit rejects amount<=0; withdraw rejects amount<=0 or amount>balance; settle returns the new balance on Ok and the unchanged old balance on Err. The model TRIED to make >=0 SMT-proven via `vera verify` (Int and Nat payload) but the verifier emits a spurious counterexample on any ensures over a match-bodied function (reports result=0 as a counterexample to result>=0); an isolated identity fn does verify ensures(@Nat.result>=0). So under Pattern A the invariant ended up hand-structured, not Z3-proven.
raw_error_excerpts: [E005] Unexpected '/' ... Expected: ABILITY, DATA, EFFECT, FN, FORALL, IMPORT, MODULE, PRIVATE (// not a comment; -- and /* */ are)
[E005] fn main() Unexpected ')' Expected: AT, __ANON_0 (params required; __ANON_0 opaque)
[E001] Function is missing its contract block. Fix: requires(true)/ensures(@Int.result >= 0)/effects(pure) { ... }
[E130] Cannot resolve @Int.1: only 1 Int binding(s) in scope (valid indices: 0..0)
[E500] Postcondition does not hold in 'settle'. Counterexample: @Int.0=0, @Outcome.0=Ok(0), @Int.result=0 (spurious; verifier can't propagate ensures through match)
A.toolchain_notes: vera 0.0.160 (Z3 bundled). vera check/run/verify. NO SKILL.md/grammar/examples read. INTEGRITY NOTE (self-reported by worker): a leftover /tmp/probe.vera (not created by the worker) containing a Vera example was inadvertently cat'd once; the worker states it had already derived @Type params/public fn/contract clauses from compiler errors before that and treated the compiler's own E001 worked-example as source of truth. Recorded here for honesty.

B.error_quality: structured - Result<Nat,String> with descriptive Err messages; E130 slot-index errors precise; CodegenSkip messages explain what the runtime-check codegen cannot lower.
B.notes: Invariant enforcement = LANGUAGE-GUARANTEED (type-level + Z3), reached from the guide WITHOUT being told to use contracts. Final attempt_2.vera carries the balance as a Nat (non-negative by construction) through deposit/withdraw/balance, with Ok payloads typed Nat. `vera verify` (Z3, Tier 1) ACTUALLY RAN and proved 42 verification conditions, including the Int->Nat non-negativity obligations on Ok(@Nat.0 + @Int.0) / Ok(@Nat.0 - @Int.0) and the balance() postcondition. attempt_1.vera additionally had richer hand-written postconditions relating Ok balance to old+/-amount and asserting errors leave input unchanged; those ALSO passed vera verify (14 verified) but could not be lowered by the `vera run` runtime-check codegen (CodegenSkip: match scrutinee WASM type), so attempt_2 relaxed them to ensures(true) to stay runnable while keeping the Nat-type guarantee. Net: the >=0 invariant is a language-verified property (type-level, Z3-discharged), not merely a hand-written runtime check.

failed_cases: E130 slot-index, then CodegenSkip on match-in-postcondition under vera run; vera check+verify passed (14 verified)

raw_error_excerpts: [E130] Cannot resolve @Nat.1: only 1 Nat binding(s) in scope (valid indices: 0..0)
vera.skip.CodegenSkip: could not infer match scrutinee WASM type (vera run; vera check & verify still passed)
attempt_2 vera run: PASS 14/14 ; vera verify: OK / 42 verified (Tier 1)
B.toolchain_notes: vera 0.0.160 (Z3 bundled). Guide source that worked: curl -fsSL https://veralang.dev/SKILL.md (network fetch succeeded in container). vera check / vera run / vera verify (42 verified Tier 1 on attempt_2; balance VERIFIED, deposit/withdraw/main trivial-or-ADT-param SKIPPED). Z3 bundled, no extra install.

reverify.command: vera run attempt_2.vera ; vera verify attempt_2.vera
